title: (BUG) wallet/db.rs: spent_note_ids_in() issues one DB query per note — O(n) round trips on large witness map
description: spent_note_ids_in() loops over each note_id and issues a separate 'SELECT note_id FROM notes WHERE note_id = ? AND spent = 1' query. The scan loop calls this once per block to prune the in-memory witness map. With a wallet holding 10,000 notes this is 10,000 round trips per block, serialized on the single-connection pool. This creates a 30-second block-stream timeout risk and a performance cliff after moderate wallet use. Fix: use a single 'SELECT note_id FROM notes WHERE note_id IN (?, ?, ...) AND spent = 1' query with all IDs bound at once, or use a temporary table approach.
